Title: Wisma Putra confirms 59 M'sians safe following Mindanao quake
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Foreign Ministry has confirmed that all 59 Malaysian nationals registered with the Consulate General of Malaysia in Davao City, Philippines, are currently safe following the 7.8 magnitude earthquake that struck southern Mindanao at 7.37 am on Monday.
The ministry also known as Wisma Putra in its media advisory said the Consulate General of Malaysia in Davao City had successfully established contact with Malaysian nationals residing in General Santos City, confirming that they are currently safe.
"At present, there are 59 Malaysian nationals officially registered with the Consulate General in Davao City. The Consulate General remains in close contact with the relevant Philippine authorities to closely monitor the safety of the wider Malaysian community in the region," the statement stated.
According to the statement, Malaysians residing in or travelling to the affected regions are strongly advised to remain vigilant, move to higher ground immediately if located near the coast, and strictly follow all safety and evacuation directives issued by the Philippine authorities.
The Foreign Ministry, through the Consulate General of Malaysia in Davao City and the Embassy of Malaysia in Manila, is continuously monitoring the developments. Local authorities have issued a tsunami warning and reported ongoing aftershocks across the affected areas.
The statement said in response to the disaster, authorities in Davao City have mandated the closure of all schools and government offices. Furthermore, reports indicate that multiple buildings in General Santos City have sustained significant structural damage, including the collapse of several structures.
Additionally, the Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) has announced the cancellation of multiple domestic flights, particularly involving routes to and from General Santos, Manila, Cebu, and Iloilo, as authorities conduct safety assessments at affected airports.
"Malaysians whose travel plans are impacted by these disruptions are advised to coordinate directly with their respective airlines for flight updates and rebooking arrangements," the statement said.
According to Wisma Putra, to facilitate timely communication and consular assistance, Malaysians are urged to register and regularly update their contact details via the e-Konsular portal at https://ekonsular.kln.gov.my
For immediate consular assistance or emergencies, Malaysian citizens may contact the duty officer at the Consulate General of Malaysia in Davao City at the Emergency Helpline: +63 951 816 6125
Should the Consulate General be unreachable due to high volume or connectivity issues during this critical period, the Embassy of Malaysia in Manila may be contacted as a supporting point of communication through the following channels: Telephone (general enquiries): +63 2 8662 8200, Emergency Helpline (after office hours): +63 961 077 8113 or email: mwmanila@kln.gov.my.
The Foreign Ministry will provide updates if needed, the statement said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah driving sustainable, digital tourism to guard against external shocks
Article:
TUARAN: The Sabah government is blending sustainability and digital innovation to build a resilient, high-value tourism industry capable of mitigating external economic shocks.
State Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said such measures were taken as external factors could affect Sabah's tourism sector.
"This approach will balance economic growth with environmental and cultural preservation," he said when officiating a hotel here yesterday.
Hajiji said he believed Sabah was on track to achieve its targeted four million visitor arrivals for Visit Malaysia 2026 and to prepare for Visit Sabah 2027. Last year, Sabah performed strongly, reaching 3.79 million visitor arrivals, an increase of 20.8 per cent compared with the previous year.
"This has generated RM8.74 billion in revenue and created nearly 400,000 jobs," he said.
Hajiji said the state government, through the Tourism, Culture and Environment Ministry, was currently drafting a comprehensive, long-term Sabah Tourism Blueprint expected to be completed by the end of this year. He added the blueprint would chart a new course for tourism growth with clear direction, address sustainability and challenges such as air connectivity, reflect local aspirations, and provide a framework beyond previous master plans.
Meanwhile, he said the newly launched Hilton Garden Inn would form part of a larger investment commitment estimated at RM500 million, covering current and future Hilton developments here.
"This will further strengthen investor confidence in the state's tourism and hospitality sector and is certainly aligned with the state government's continuing efforts to strengthen Sabah's tourism industry," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: WWF-Malaysia partners with Sabah coastal communities to protect sharks, rays
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: WWF-Malaysia is working with coastal communities in Sabah to conserve sharks and rays amid concerns over declining populations.
In a survey conducted between November 2023 and December 2024, WWF-Malaysia and Universiti Malaysia Sabah counted, measured and recorded sharks and rays brought ashore at fish markets and landing sites across Kota Kinabalu and Sandakan.
The research, approved and supported by the Sabah Fisheries Department, recorded 1,748 sharks and 2,890 rays representing 34 shark species and 45 ray species, respectively.
"Early analyses show that at least 80 per cent of sharks and 93 per cent of rays captured were immature, caught before they could reproduce.
"This is particularly concerning as these animals take 12 to 15 years to reach reproductive age, meaning every early death is a significant blow to their populations," a WWF-Malaysia statement said.
Sharks and rays play an important role in maintaining marine ecosystems by regulating prey populations, stirring up sediments and cycling nutrients.
WWF-Malaysia senior marine programme officer Serena Adam said the organisation was now working closely with coastal communities following the findings.
"Not just to monitor incidental catches but to build awareness, buy-in, and capacity for live release of juveniles," she said.
Through a pilot programme led by WWF-Malaysia, 13 fishers in Tun Mustapha Park volunteered to release juvenile sharks and rays caught alive.
Of the 49 sharks and rays recorded by participating fishers, 61 per cent were landed alive and successfully released back into the sea. The released species included bamboo sharks, catsharks, whitespotted whiprays, Jenkins' whiprays and smaller stingray species.
WWF-Malaysia head of marine Chitra Devi said the engagement with fishing communities went beyond raising awareness.
"Our officers sit with fishers in focus group discussions, hearing firsthand how shark and ray populations have changed over the years.
"By placing trained enumerators within villages, we're not just gathering data, we're building trust and empowering communities with knowledge that strengthens conservation from the ground up," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirAsia introduces new procedures for specialised child restraint devices
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: AirAsia has introduced enhanced procedures for medically approved child restraint devices (CRDs) and submitted a new standard operating procedure (SOP) to the relevant authorities for approval.
This follows a review of an incident involving a family travelling from
Singapore
to Kuching last month.
The airline said the review identified an opportunity to strengthen existing processes relating to the assessment of medically approved CRDs and other specialised travel requirements.
AirAsia X chief safety and quality officer Captain Saravanan Subramaniam said the enhanced procedures were designed to better support guests with special needs while maintaining safety requirements.
"We recognise that every guest's circumstances and requirements are unique.
"This incident has prompted us to enhance our processes to better support guests with specialised needs.
"We are committed to ensuring our policies continue to evolve to meet the diverse needs of those we serve" he said in a statement today.
Following the review, the company developed enhanced acceptance procedures that are more inclusive, which have been submitted to the relevant authorities for approval, he said.
The airline also apologised to the family involved, saying it regretted the inconvenience caused when they were unable to travel on flight AK1776 on May 26.
"The safety and well-being of our guests will always remain our highest priority. We sincerely apologise to the family for the inconvenience caused.
"Although the decision was made in accordance with the applicable safety requirements and operating procedures in place at the time, it was never our intention to prevent any guest from travelling," Saravanan said.
AirAsia said guests travelling with specialised needs are encouraged to refer to its support page for the latest travel requirements and guidance.
The airline added that it remains committed to making air travel accessible and inclusive for all guests. It will continue improving clarity, consistency and the travel experience for passengers with specialised needs.
The review follows an incident involving a family travelling on flight AK1776 from Singapore to Kuching on May 26.
NST previously reported that the family was unable to board the flight after concerns were raised over a specialised child restraint device used by their daughter, who has quadriplegic cerebral palsy.
Following the incident, AirAsia announced a
review
of its policies and procedures relating to specialised child restraint devices.
#kw: AirAsia, AK1776, child restraint device, cerebral palsy, aviation, safety, accessibility, inclusive, travel, SOP, Kuching, Singapore, nst, news, malaysia, boarding
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Millions in prizes lure World Cup betting syndicates, punters
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: International gambling syndicates and local bookies are using lucrative cash prizes running into millions of ringgit to lure punters into placing bets for the 2026 World Cup, which kicks off this Thursday.
With illegal football betting increasingly shifting to digital platforms, authorities are bracing for a surge in online gambling activity linked to the tournament, as syndicates exploit offshore websites, mobile applications and cross-border financial networks to evade detection.
City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said online gambling applications are expected to be the preferred platform for bettors, allowing wagers on everything from match results and winning teams to first goalscorers, penalty outcomes and even the first corner kick.
"In previous World Cups, punters would gather at public places such as food courts and restaurants to place bets. Today, advances in technology have shifted gambling activities to digital platforms, allowing bets to be placed privately from home.
"Police anticipate an increase in illegal gambling and betting activities during the 2026 World Cup, particularly through online platforms. Historically, every major sporting event such as the World Cup has led to a spike in illegal betting due to heightened public interest," he told Berita Harian.
To curb the activity, Fadil said Kuala Lumpur police are prepared to work closely with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission to identify and take action against gambling applications and platforms promoted by bookies ahead of the tournament.
The trend mirrors findings from several international crackdowns coordinated by Interpol under Operation Soga, which targets illegal football betting and related money laundering activities.
Past operations revealed that syndicates increasingly rely on websites, mobile applications, online banking accounts and e-wallets to manage large-scale betting operations rather than conventional gambling dens.
During Operation Soga X, conducted in conjunction with the UEFA Euro 2024 tournament, authorities worldwide arrested more than 5,100 individuals and seized criminal proceeds exceeding US$59 million (RM237 million).
Investigations uncovered syndicates operating through computer servers hosted in multiple countries and extensive networks of bank accounts and e-wallets used to collect bets and process winnings.
Based on patterns identified in previous international operations, football betting syndicates typically operate through multiple layers to shield their masterminds from law enforcement.
In some cases, individuals are paid to open online bank accounts used to channel gambling proceeds before funds are transferred to the syndicate, complicating efforts to trace money flows and freeze assets.
In Malaysia, the police's Op Soga IX during the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar resulted in the arrest of 152 individuals suspected of involvement in illegal football betting. Authorities also seized cash, mobile phones, computers and betting software credit worth more than RM3 million.
Fadil said the Criminal Investigation Department would intensify surveillance, intelligence-gathering and enforcement operations against illegal gambling syndicates and bookies throughout the World Cup period.
"Monitoring efforts will focus heavily on online gambling activities, as syndicates now primarily operate through digital platforms," he said.
Cases involving illegal gambling are investigated under the Betting Act 1953, the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953 and other relevant laws.
Under existing legislation, those convicted of operating or participating in illegal gambling activities may face fines and imprisonment, while offences involving online betting platforms and electronic communication devices carry heavier penalties.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court of Appeal upholds 36-year jail term for five over sword murder
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal today upheld the 36-year prison sentence and 12 strokes of the cane imposed on five men, including two sets of brothers, for the murder of a man who was slashed with a sword.
A three-judge panel comprising Datuk Azman Abdullah, Datuk Hayatul Akmal Abdul Aziz and Datuk Mohd Radzi Abdul Hamid unanimously dismissed the appellants' bid to overturn their convictions and sentences, which were handed down by the Kuala Terengganu High Court on May 9, 2024.
The five men are Aizz Amidie Aziz, 32; brothers Mohd Rizalman Awang, 36, and Zahaman Fairol, 39; as well as brothers Mohd Uwais Mat Nawi, 31, and Mohd Hafify, 30.
They were found guilty of murdering Mohd Shahrulanuar Dek Der, 22, in a swampy area at Kampung Pengkalan Nyireh, Kampung Raja, Besut, between 4.40pm and 8.30pm on June 21, 2017, under Section 302 of the Penal Code read with Section 34.
In delivering the judgment, Justice Azman said the appeals had no merit as the prosecution's witnesses, including a protected witness, had consistently testified that all the appellants were involved in stabbing the victim.
He said the protected witness, who was an eyewitness hiding in nearby bushes during the incident, had given a detailed account of what transpired.
"The witness's refusal to answer certain questions that could reveal his identity did not affect the prosecution's case. The trial judge was also satisfied that the testimony was supported by other prosecution witnesses," he said.
Justice Azman added that the trial judge had also properly evaluated the defence's case and found the claim that another individual committed the stabbing to be an afterthought.
He said the High Court judge was correct in concluding that the appellants had failed to raise a reasonable doubt at the end of their defence case, and therefore there was no reason for appellate intervention.
Three of the men – Aizz Amidie, Mohd Rizalman and Zahaman Fairol – were ordered to serve their sentences from the date of their arrest on July 11, 2017, while the remaining two will have their sentences backdated to Aug 11, the same year.
Earlier, defence counsel Datuk Hisyam Teh Poh Teik sought a reduction of the sentence to 30 years from the date of arrest, but deputy public prosecutor Mohd Zain Ibrahim argued for the sentence to be maintained to serve as a deterrent.
Reports previously stated that the victim was killed following a dispute linked to the burning of a motorcycle belonging to a relative of one of the accused.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nvidia unveils AI infrastructure deals in South Korea
Article:
SEOUL: US chip titan Nvidia announced on Monday a raft of artificial intelligence deals in South Korea, where booming business for semiconductor companies is fuelling debate over how much of the profits go to workers.
Nvidia, the world's most valuable company, unveiled with SK Telecom a "gigawatt-scale" AI data centre construction project, with the first facility set to come online in 2027.
It will support "AI services for enterprises and industries across Korea, with the vision to expand to greater Asia regions", the companies said.
No figure was given for how much the pair will invest in the data centres, or for other new tie-ups that Nvidia touted with the likes of Naver, LG Group, Hyundai and Doosan Group, including on AI robotics.
The deals were unveiled after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang spent the weekend eating barbecue and fried chicken with the country's tech leaders in Seoul and appearing on a popular TV show.
Nvidia also said it would work with chipmaker SK hynix to develop advanced memory components that are needed to run AI systems but are currently in short supply.
Their "multi-year technology partnership" will "sustain the global buildout of AI factories" by supporting supply for advanced memory chips, they said.
Lian Jye Su, a chief analyst at Omdia, told AFP that Nvidia's new deals were "about strengthening existing relationships and further validating South Korea's role in the global AI supply chain."
- 'Please make more' -
Governments and companies are pouring hundreds of billions of dollars into AI infrastructure, creating a global memory chip shortage.
That has sent profits skyrocketing for manufacturers such as SK hynix and rival Samsung Electronics, whose union recently agreed a deal with management on bonuses.
The chair of SK Group, parent of SK Telecom and SK hynix, vowed last week to double production capacity of the silicon wafers used to make memory chips.
But Chey Tae-won also reiterated his prediction that shortages could persist until 2030.
Huang signed a memory chip display at the SK hynix booth at last week's Computex trade show in Taipei, writing: "Please make more."
Asked in Taipei about the labour dispute at Samsung, Huang said companies should pay workers "as much as possible."
Under the union deal, struck to avert a strike, around 60 per cent of Samsung's domestic workforce is eligible to receive a bonus of roughly US$330,000 this year, based on a market estimate of operating profit.
South Korean President Lee Jae Myung weighed in on Monday on the debate about how to share the gains from AI-driven growth.
The topic is "very difficult" but "an unavoidable reality" in the AI era, Lee told reporters, adding that it will eventually require international discussion.
"If the operating profit margin exceeds 75 per cent, does it belong solely to the company?" Lee said.
He asked whether workers, investors, the state that has invested heavily in research and development, and taxpayers who supported it also deserve a share.
However, Lee cautioned that any attempt to redistribute excess corporate profits could discourage investment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penampang LPG cylinder fire: Man suffers 80pct burns
Article:
PENAMPANG: A 29-year-old man suffered burns to about 80 per cent of his body after a liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cylinder caught fire in the kitchen of an apartment unit in Taman Penampang today.
Penampang Fire and Rescue Station chief Zeno Tingalan said firefighters received a distress call at 11.15am and dispatched six personnel, a fire engine and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit to the scene.
"The team arrived at 11.22am and found an LPG cylinder on fire in the apartment's kitchen.
"The victim sustained burns to about 80 per cent of his body as a result of the incident," he told Harian Metro when contacted.
Zeno said firefighters administered initial treatment before the victim was taken to hospital for further care.
He said the team also carried out checks to ensure there were no further hazards at the scene before the operation concluded at 11.35am.
The cause of the fire and the extent of the losses are being investigated.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Balfour Beatty's US military housing unit exits government-mandated oversight
Article:
NEW YORK: US federal government-mandated oversight over a Balfour Beatty unit has ended, the British construction group said on Monday, almost five years after the military housing subsidiary had pleaded guilty to defrauding the US military.
To continue embedding improvements as part of the company's long-term operating model while maintaining focus on operational performance and the resident experience.
Balfour Beatty Communities, which manages housing for US service members and their families, pleaded guilty in 2021 to fraud after falsifying maintenance records to boost company returns and agreed to pay over US$65 million in fines and restitution.
The US Department of Justice required the company to hire an independent third-party corporate monitor as a condition of the settlement, tasking the third party with reviewing compliance policies to ensure the violations were remedied.
The monitorship, originally extended to June 6 to allow more time for remediation work, ended on schedule after the company implemented enhanced reporting mechanisms, stronger leadership structures and more robust compliance processes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Entire civil service machinery to be mobilised against visa abuse - Chief secretary
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is mobilising its entire public service machinery to crack down on foreign nationals misusing visas, permits and licences for business activities in Malaysia, said Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
The move, he said, follows Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's directive for firm, sweeping action to enforce the law and protect the nation's economic sovereignty.
Beyond legal compliance, Shamsul Azri explained, the effort aims to safeguard the interests of Malaysians and uphold a fair, transparent and integrity-driven business ecosystem.
"I take the Prime Minister's directive seriously. I am mobilising the entire public service machinery to strengthen coordinated, integrated action across ministries and agencies, to enforce the law, protect the people, and preserve a just business environment, " he declared in a Facebook post today.
He added that there will be no compromise on any form of visa, permit or licence abuse that threatens the national economy, public welfare or Malaysia's sovereign interests.
Earlier, during his speech at the Ministry of Communications' monthly assembly, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim voiced concern over the influx of foreigners misusing visitor and student visas to conduct business locally.
He said some foreigners run businesses using licences and permits belonging to locals, while others register legally but source goods and labour from their home countries, directly undercutting local businesses and communities.
To combat this, Anwar has instructed all relevant ministries and agencies, including the Inland Revenue Board, Royal Malaysian Customs Department, Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living, local authorities, the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission and Bank Negara Malaysia to take firm, comprehensive action against such activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH targets over 20 seats in NS election
Article:
SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan is targeting to win more than 20 state seats in the upcoming state election to secure a clear majority.
State DAP chairman Anthony Loke said the target was based on the coalition's achievements in the 14th general election, where it won 20 seats to form the state government.
He said component parties were committed to helping their PH allies defend the state government and obtain a strong mandate from voters.
"The Negri Sembilan DAP committee meeting today discussed preparations for the Negri Sembilan state election after the state legislative assembly was dissolved last Friday.
"We are ready to face the election and the target of Negri Sembilan PH is to win at least more than 20 seats in this state election," he said after chairing the meeting here today.
Loke, who is also the Seremban member of parliament, announced that he would defend the Chennah state seat.
He also confirmed that all DAP incumbents were ready to defend the 11 seats won by the party in the 15th state election.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan state legislative assembly effective June 5 to pave the way for the state election, which must be held within 60 days.
In the 2023 Negri Sembilan election, PH and BN formed the state government after controlling 31 of the 36 state seats.
PH won 17 seats while BN secured 14, with Perikatan Nasional winning five.
Negri Sembilan became the second state to dissolve its assembly to make way for a state election after Johor did so on June 1. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar to outline Malaysia's geopolitical vision at Nikkei conference in Japan
Article:
TOKYO: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to deliver a keynote address at the 31st Nikkei International Conference, highlighting Malaysia's narrative in the global geopolitical landscape.
Malaysia's ambassador to Japan Datuk Shahril Effendi Abd Ghany said Anwar's address, titled "Navigating Strategic Uncertainty", will focus on Malaysia's role in navigating geopolitical complexities while exploring new opportunities for regional prosperity.
"The prime minister's keynote address will further underscore Malaysia's influence on the international stage, which always plays an active role in regional and global issues.
"At the same time, it will strengthen Malaysia's profile and image as a progressive and resilient nation committed to regional and global cooperation," he said.
He said this at a media briefing at the Malaysian Embassy here ahead of Anwar's three-day official visit to Japan, which begins today.
Shahril said the Nikkei conference brings together government leaders, corporate figures and experts from around the world to discuss Asia's economic and geopolitical direction.
In addition to the forum, Anwar is also scheduled to deliver a special lecture at the University of Tokyo titled "Humanity in a Human-Machine Civilisation", focusing on the balance between technological advancement and human values.
"The special lecture will highlight the importance of maintaining human values in national economic development, particularly at a time when artificial intelligence is advancing rapidly.
"It is in line with the Malaysia Madani concept introduced by the prime minister.
"University of Tokyo president Professor Teruo Fujii is scheduled to pay a courtesy call on Anwar before the lecture," he said.
Anwar is on a three-day official visit to Japan from today until June 10.
The finance minister is also scheduled to attend a roundtable session with selected Japanese business leaders and industry players, as well as meetings with companies based here.
The sessions will showcase Malaysia's strengths as an investment destination and trading partner, as part of efforts to attract high-quality Japanese investments and strengthen trade ties and economic cooperation between the two countries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MOT yet to decide on diesel vehicle road tax cut
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Transport Ministry has yet to announce a decision on the proposed reduction in road tax rates for diesel-powered vehicles.
Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said any decision on the matter would be subject to the outcome of discussions at the National Economic Action Council (NEAC).
"The matter is still under discussion. We need to wait for the outcome of the council meeting and related deliberations, which are held every Tuesday.
"We cannot make any announcement at this stage. Any decision will be based on the outcome of the council meeting," he told reporters after the International Rail Transit Forum (IRTF) 2026 here today.
He was responding to questions on the latest developments regarding the proposed reduction in road tax rates following adjustments to diesel prices.
During the Road Transport Department's 80th anniversary celebration recently, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government was considering reducing
road tax for diesel vehicles
to cushion the impact of rising fuel costs.
He said the matter had been discussed with Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook, with the ministry tasked to work out the details for implementation in the near term.
Loke had recently said the ministry was studying suitable
mechanisms
to implement the proposal, including assessing its potential impact on government revenue.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Private jet market defies Iran war gloom as rich flock to Monaco GP, Cannes
Article:
LONDON/PARIS/GDANSK: As soaring jet fuel prices triggered by the Iran war send ripples across the global travel market, a wealthy elite of CEOs, celebrities and sports stars is flying by private jet in greater numbers than ever before, to glitzy events from the Monaco Grand Prix to the Cannes film festival.
The phenomenon is another sign of the so-called "K-shaped" economy that is showing up across consumer markets from luxury to dining, industry watchers say, as high-income travellers spend more while middle- and lower-income groups tighten their belts, with budget carriers in particular feeling the squeeze.
Jet fuel costs have roughly doubled since the start of the war in late February, forcing global airlines to cancel flights and raise ticket prices, while missile and drone strikes around the Gulf have seen flights almost halve in a region that was a global connection hub.
"The world is in turmoil, but not our passengers," Deniz Weissenborn, owner of Platoon Aviation, which charters eight-seat jets, told Reuters, explaining that its clients are wealthy enough to absorb higher prices.
"If you fly in a private jet, I don't think you're bothered by an increase of 1,000 or 2,000 euros."
According to aviation data firm WINGX, the number of private flights has increased by about 4.0 per cent globally so far this year, adding thousands of trips. In the same period, overall global capacity has fallen 3-4 per cent, data from aviation analytics firm Cirium shows.
'AS BUSY AS EVER'
Private jet pilots and executives told Reuters that charter jet services are seeing an uptick in bookings as wealthy travellers turn away from premium, business and first class in an effort to dodge the risk of commercial flight cancellations and airport disruption due to the conflict. Amalfi Jets founder and CEO Kolin Jones said there had been around a quarter more requests for Cannes this year compared to last, while those for Sunday's Monaco GP were up almost a third, as people switched up from commercial flights. "Lots who could afford it but flew commercial are now happy to pay more for the safer option," Jones said. "Cannes Film Festival, Monaco Grand Prix, and World Cup-related travel from Europe to the US are driving demand."
Eight private jet executives said that while private traffic to the Middle East had dipped given airspace safety concerns, demand for travel to Europe and the United States was likely to approach record levels this year.
"It is as busy as ever," said Andy Spencer, a private jet pilot who has flown routes in the Middle East and Asia.
During early February's US Super Bowl in California, private traffic at nearby airports was three times that of a normal day, WINGX told Reuters. For April's Masters Golf Tournament in Augusta, private traffic was 10 times more than normal, jumping from fewer than 50 flights to more than 400.
"Our customers' flight hours continue to hit record highs month after month," private jet maker Embraer's CEO Francisco Gomes Neto told Reuters at an executive aviation airshow in May in Sao Paulo, Brazil.
'PEOPLE FEEL SAFE WHEN THEY HAVE CONTROL'
Private jets have come in for criticism from climate groups and campaigners, who say their use underscores global inequality, is a threat to the environment and that regulation of the sector is too lax.
A spokesperson for the European Business Aviation Association said the sector played an important role in Europe's connectivity and criticism was overly simplistic, while manufacturers and charter operators added that well-heeled individuals were simply looking for more security in uncertain times.
"Every time there are world events, private aviation gets a little bit of a bump, every single time," said Jason Middleton, owner of Silver Air Private Jets, citing the Iran war, the Covid pandemic and unrest in South America.
"It's like a safety thing...People feel safe when they have control."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: More than 30 left homeless after fire destroys 10 homes in Kampung Cina
Article:
KOTA BARU: An ordinary Monday afternoon turned into a nightmare for residents of Jalan Kampung Cina when a fire swept through 10 houses here today, leaving more than 30 people homeless.
No casualties were reported in the blaze, which broke out at about noon.
Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Wan Mohd Zain Wan Yusof said the department received an emergency call at 12.34pm and dispatched 20 firefighters from the Kota Baru and Kota Darul Naim fire stations to the scene.
"Firefighters arrived at the scene five minutes later and found 10 houses engulfed in flames.
"They used three fire engines and two water tankers in the operation," he said.
At press time, firefighters were still carrying out extinguishing and overhaul works at the scene.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jaslinda loses nearly 10kg after 14-day ordeal, may go home this week
Article:
TAPAH: Jaslinda Saludin, who survived 14 days alone in the wilderness near Gunung Batu Putih, is recovering well and is expected to be discharged from hospital this week after losing nearly 10kg during the ordeal.
Tapah Hospital director Dr Wan Immi Salim said Jaslinda had shown significant improvement since being admitted on Friday night following her rescue.
"There is a noticeable difference between when she went missing and when she was found. We estimate she may have lost close to 10kg.
"However, her condition is stable and she continues to improve. She is able to eat, speak and communicate normally," she told reporters at the hospital today.
Also present were state Human Resources, Health, Indian Community Affairs and National Integration Committee chairman Datuk A. Sivanesan, and state Communications, Multimedia and NGO Committee chairman Mohd Azlan Helmi.
Dr Immi said the 49-year-old hiker was expected to remain under observation for a few more days before being allowed to return home.
"If her recovery continues as expected, she may be discharged within this week," she said.
Jaslinda was admitted to Tapah Hospital on Friday after being brought out of the forest by the Fire and Rescue Department's Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) team.
She is being treated for wounds and abrasions sustained during her ordeal, as well as a leg injury that required a partial cast.
Despite reports that she had fallen several times while trying to find her way out of the forest, Dr Immi said medical examinations found no evidence of head or brain injuries.
"When we first treated her, she informed us that she had fallen several times, especially in the early stages of her ordeal. She reportedly fell about five metres into a ravine, which caused her to lose her bearings and become disoriented.
"Because of these incidents, we conducted thorough examinations to rule out any head or brain injuries.
"So far, Alhamdulillah, there is no evidence of any injury to her head or brain. The only injuries identified are external wounds and abrasions," she said.
She added that Jaslinda was particularly happy to be reunited with her husband, children and other family members after the traumatic experience.
Asked whether the hospital was providing counselling services, Dr Immi said the facility did not have an in-house counsellor.
"However, our ward staff regularly check on her well-being, and I personally ask her whether she is able to rest, sleep and cope with her situation.
"Alhamdulillah, everything has been very positive. She is a very positive woman with a strong will. I believe that has been a major factor in her remarkably fast recovery," she said.
After two weeks missing in Gunung Batu Putih here, Jaslinda was
found safe
at an Orang Asli settlement near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu in Pos Musoh on Friday.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon said she was found about 3pm by a resident, Nazri Bah Eng, who later informed his nephew in Johor, who alerted the authorities.
Jaslinda
had joined a group of 13 hikers and two Forestry Department mountain guides for the Trans Spencer Chapman expedition, which began at 2am on May 23 via the Pos Gedung–Gunung Bah Gading–Gunung Batu Putih–Kuala Woh route.
She and another hiker, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, had earlier experienced health issues and stopped the climb.
However, Jaslinda later continued the ascent and was last seen by mountain guides at 7.30am on May 24.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sungai Petani crash: MOT awaits probe into accident that killed six
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Transport Ministry is awaiting the outcome of a full investigation into a crash that claimed the lives of six family members in Sungai Petani yesterday.
Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the ministry would leave it to the relevant authorities to complete their investigation before commenting on the cause of the tragedy.
He said the ministry was still waiting for a comprehensive report and would not speculate on any possible factors, including the condition of the newly purchased Proton X50 sport utility vehicle (SUV) involved in the crash.
"We are still waiting for the investigation. I cannot comment at this stage and do not want to make any premature conclusions.
"I cannot say more than that," he told reporters after attending the International Rail Transit Forum (IRTF) 2026 here today.
He was responding to questions on whether a vehicle-related issue could have contributed to the fatal accident.
Yesterday,
six members of a family were killed
when the newly purchased SUV they were travelling in collided with a lorry around 3.50pm.
The victims were identified as a married couple, their child, the driver's mother, younger sibling and nephew.
One of the victims was a police officer attached to the Seberang Prai Selatan district police headquarters in Penang.
It is understood that the Proton X50 carrying the victims had been collected from a dealership in Juru, Penang, just hours before the crash.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi wants an AI prompt engineer in every digital community centre
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Communications Ministry aims to produce at least one prompt engineer at every National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) nationwide as part of efforts to strengthen artificial intelligence (AI) literacy and skills among young Malaysians.
Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the initiative goes beyond exposing youths to AI tools, focusing instead on equipping them with the expertise to develop applications that can address local community needs.
"I would like to set a clear aspiration that we will work towards producing a prompt engineer at every Nadi centre across the country, whether in Teluk Ramunia in Johor or Pulau Banggi in Sabah.
"We want young people who understand not only how to use Gemini, Copilot and other AI platforms, but also how to harness these technologies to develop new applications that can help solve problems on the ground," he said at the ministry's monthly assembly today, which was graced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Present were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching and Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah.
Fahmi said the initiative could help nurture a new generation of technologically skilled local talent while creating employment opportunities outside major urban centres.
Meanwhile, he also called on the ministry to expand its community outreach programmes in collaboration with other ministries and agencies to ensure more Malaysians benefit from government services.
He said the initiative would begin from June 12 to 14 in conjunction with activities surrounding the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with active participation from agencies including the Department of Broadcasting Malaysia (RTM) and the Department of Information Malaysia (Japen).
"I want RTM and Japen to intensify programmes on the ground. Broadcast activities, especially on weekends, and live programmes should be organised in various locations," he said.
Fahmi added that ministry initiatives such as outdoor cinema screenings, community feasts and the Safe Internet Campaign should be expanded with the support of other government agencies.
According to him, the integrated approach would allow communities to access a wider range of government services, activities and programmes through a single, community-focused platform.
As such, he has directed that planning and coordination be finalised immediately to enable implementation to begin this weekend.
– BERNAMA
TAGS: NST, news, Malaysia, Fahmi Fadzil, prompt engineer, AI, NADI, FIFA World Cup 2026
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PLKN to discuss voting arrangements if Johor, Negri polls clash
Article:
PEKAN: The National Service Training Department will discuss with the Election Commission (EC) on suitable voting arrangements for trainees if the Johor or Negeri Sembilan elections coincide with the training programme.
The third series of the National Service Training Programme (PLKN) 3.0 is scheduled to run from July 11 to Aug 24, while the EC is set to meet on June 12 to discuss key dates for the 16th Johor state election.
Its director-general, Major-General Datuk Marzuki Mokhtar, said that if polling day falls during the training programme, the department will explore suitable options, including postal or early voting.
"We might adopt a military-style voting arrangement, as the recruits will be at the camp for training. It could be either postal or early voting, but that depends on the EC's approval.
"If the EC requires the trainees to be physically present to cast their ballots, then we will look into the necessary arrangements," he told reporters at the 505th Territorial Army Regiment Camp here today.
He was asked about the department's arrangements should the Johor or Negri Sembilan elections be held during the 45-day
training programme
next month.
Earlier, he witnessed the passing-out parade of 531 male trainees who began their training on April 25.
The minimum voting age was lowered to 18 from 21 after the Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2019 was passed by the Dewan Rakyat in 2019.
The PLKN programme involves participants aged 18 and above undergoing training at army camps for 45 days.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teacher claims trial to 14 counts of sexually assaulting six students
Article:
KLANG: A school teacher pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to 14 charges of sexual assault involving six of his 15-year-old female students from a secondary school in Sabak Bernam.
Idi Johan Mohd Zohdi, 45, entered his pleas after the charges were read before judge Sharifah Hascindie Syed Omar.
"I understand the charges. I want to go on trial," he said in reply to each count.
His wife, who is also a teacher, and his siblings were present in the public gallery during the proceedings.
On all charges, the father of one, who teaches mathematics and music, allegedly committed physical sexual assault against the students at several locations, including classrooms, a house, a music storeroom and a car around Sabak Bernam between January last year and February.
The charges were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act, punishable under Section 14(b), which carries a maximum 20-year jail term and whipping.
The charges were read together with Section 16(1) of the same act, relating to offences committed in a relationship of trust as a teacher, which provides for an additional jail term of up to five years and a minimum two strokes of the cane.
Deputy public prosecutor Nor Suhaili Abu Bakar did not offer bail as the offences were non-bailable.
"In all cases, the victims were his students.
"If the court exercises its discretion to grant bail, it should be set at RM20,000 for each charge, with conditions prohibiting the accused from harassing the victims and requiring him to report to the police station monthly," she said.
Lawyer Datuk Mohd Imran Tamrin appealed for bail, describing his client as a long-serving teacher who has been in service since 2002 and has no prior criminal convictions or disciplinary record.
"He is committed to clearing his name and voluntarily appeared in court after being informed by the police.
"His family is also here to provide moral support," he said.
Imran argued that bail should not be excessive or punitive, stressing that his client is presumed innocent until proven guilty.
"He could potentially face suspension from work and lose his sole source of income.
"The bail sum should be reasonable and be fixed at RM30,000 for all charges," he said.
After hearing submissions from both parties, the judge granted bail of RM30,000 with one surety.
She also ordered the accused to report to the police station once a month, refrain from interfering with the victims and surrender his passport until the disposal of the case.
Imran was assisted by Mohd Hafizuddin Khan Norkhan, Ainul Aizat Ahmad Ishak and Intan Syakieraa Zakaria.
After the proceedings, Imran said the accused's family would post bail.
He also revealed that his client had been transferred from the school and was currently placed at the district education office.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cruise demand rises as geopolitical tensions reshape travel choices
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Rising geopolitical uncertainty and periodic disruptions to air travel routes are prompting some travellers to reassess long-haul flight reliance, with cruise holidays emerging as an alternative leisure option.
Industry players said demand for cruise travel has strengthened as consumers look for itineraries that reduce exposure to airspace risks, flight rerouting, and border-related disruptions linked to global tensions.
The shift comes as cruise operators and loyalty programmes expand redemption offerings to capture growing appetite for alternative holiday formats, particularly in Asia-Pacific markets such as Malaysia, Singapore and other neighbouring Southeast Asian economies.
Against this backdrop, Singapore Airlines Group's loyalty ecosystem, through KrisFlyer and travel platform partner arrivia, has broadened access to cruise bookings using frequent flyer miles.
The enhancement allows members to redeem KrisFlyer miles via KrisFlyer Cruise, arrivia's dedicated platform, across more than 30,000 itineraries operated by over 40 cruise lines.
The itineraries include major operators such as Royal Caribbean International and Disney Cruise Line, spanning destinations across Asia, Australia and global routes popular with travellers, including Malaysians.
John Williams, chief operating officer of arrivia International said cruises are gaining traction as travellers seek greater certainty in their holiday planning.
"This partnership is about giving KrisFlyer members more meaningful ways to use their miles," he said, adding that the platform offers access to thousands of sailings supported by dedicated cruise specialists.
The global cruise industry carried a record 37.2 million passengers in 2025, up 7.5 per cent year-on-year, with Asia-Pacific demand continuing to accelerate as regional consumers increasingly opt for bundled, multi-destination sea travel.
Analysts note that while air travel remains dominant, cruises are capturing incremental demand from travellers prioritising convenience, itinerary stability and all-in-one vacation experiences during periods of geopolitical volatility.
"Malaysia is expected to benefit from the trend, with its proximity to major regional cruise hubs and growing outbound middle-class travel demand supporting long-term expansion in cruise-related tourism activity," the analyst said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC warns against online gambling content ahead of World Cup
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) will act on complaints involving online gambling-related content ahead of the 2026 World Cup campaign.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said members of the public should lodge complaints with MCMC if they come across links or content promoting online gambling.
"As we know, online gambling is not permitted in our country. Therefore, if MCMC receives complaints, action can be taken.
"To members of the public, if you see links or content related to online gambling, please submit a complaint to MCMC so that action can be taken," he said.
He said this at a press conference after a ministerial dialogue and media roundtable on safe and responsible suicide reporting, here today.
Fahmi said similar action could also be taken against online gambling portals.
Berita Harian previously reported
that cash prizes worth millions of ringgit were being used as a major lure by international betting syndicates and local bookies to attract wagers from gamblers on matches during the 2026 World Cup, which kicks off on Thursday.
The special report revealed that betting is no longer conducted through conventional means as in the past. Since previous editions of the tournament, bookie agents and punters have increasingly collected wagers through online platforms and dozens of websites based overseas.
As a result, domestic and international enforcement agencies are monitoring the possibility of a rise in illegal football gambling activities, which are increasingly dependent on digital platforms, cross-border financial networks and false identities to evade detection by authorities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gua Tempurung tower plugs coverage gap on North-South Expressway
Article:
GOPENG: There are no stretches along the North-South Expressway (NSE) in Perak currently experiencing complete telecommunications coverage disruptions or blind spots, said State Communications, Multimedia and NGO Committee chairman Mohd Azlan Helmi.
He said this was after a new telecommunications tower in the Gua Tempurung area began operations a month ago.
Mohd Azlan said the Gua Tempurung area had previously been identified as one of the main locations experiencing telecommunications coverage disruptions, especially for users travelling through hilly stretches of road from the north and south.
"The issue (coverage disruption) has now been resolved with the cooperation of various parties, including the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), Plus Malaysia Berhad, telecommunications service providers and related agencies.
"We have placed Gua Tempurung as one of the hotspots for blind spot connectivity under the Perak State Telecommunications Coverage Issues Monitoring Committee, which was established in 2023," he told reporters after inspecting the new telecommunications tower in Gua Tempurung here today.
Mohd Azlan said that apart from Gua Tempurung, the state government had previously addressed telecommunications coverage issues in the Menora Tunnel area.
"I believe there are no longer any areas with no connectivity at all.The only areas affected now are those with weak coverage due to terrain factors or certain obstacles, but they still have telecommunications connections," he said.
According to him, the state government together with the MCMC and telecommunications service providers would continue to monitor coverage quality to ensure it complies with set standards and meets the needs of increasing highway users.
He said efforts to improve telecommunications coverage would continue from time to time, including at rest and service (R&R) areas which often experience congestion during the holiday season, as well as along the West Coast Expressway (WCE).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bear roaming Japanese city forces closure of almost 100 schools
Article:
TOKYO: A bear roaming the streets of a Japanese city for three days forced the closure of nearly 100 schools Monday, as dozens of hunters and officials searched for the animal.
The city government of Utsunomiya, north of Tokyo, closed all 94 public primary and junior high schools in the area after receiving more than 10 reports of bear sightings since Saturday – including in a shopping arcade.
"We have vehicles out to areas where a bear was seen to make people aware and to urge people to stay indoors or in vehicles," a city official told AFP, adding that dozens of hunters, police and local officials have been looking for the animal.
It was not clear whether there is one bear or more, he said, speaking on the customary condition of anonymity.
In recent years, Japan has seen an increasing number of bear sightings and attacks, especially in urban areas.
A record 13 people were killed by bears in Japan last year, and there has been a jump in encounters as the animals emerge hungry from hibernation.
In the last year to March, bear sightings nationwide topped 50,000 – more than double the previous record set two years earlier, according to official data.
In Utsunomiya, a regional capital and home to 510,000 residents, there were just two unconfirmed bear sightings in the previous year.
The bear now being hunted was first spotted Saturday morning, north of the city centre, and was described as being around one metre long.
A series of sightings followed, including in a residential neighbourhood that day, at a shopping arcade on Sunday, and at a park, a high school and a junior school.
Early Monday, residents spotted a bear close to a wholesale market, the city official said. --AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Umno Youth: Loke 'confused' between national duty and politics
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Umno Youth took a swipe at DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke, saying he is "confused between national responsibility and political choice" amid ongoing debate over cooperation between the two coalitions.
Rooted in ideological differences rather than racial sentiment, the wing defended Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's position of not cooperating or sitting at the same table with DAP in the Johor polls.
Its youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said the 'no DAP' stance reflected fundamental political divergence between the two parties and should not be mischaracterised as race-based politics.
Noor Azleen, who backed Onn Hafiz's position, said the caretaker Johor menteri besar's stand was consistent with Umno and BN's state-level political principles, particularly the decision to rule out cooperation with DAP in the upcoming election.
Strongly rejecting claims that the stance was driven by racial sentiment, he said it was normal in any democratic system for parties with differing ideologies, principles and values to opt against collaboration.
He said that ideological differences between Umno and DAP remained fundamental, pointing to DAP's continued reference to the "Malaysian Malaysia" concept in its party constitution, which he said was not aligned with the Federal Constitution framework.
Noor Azleen was responding to Loke's remarks comparing political cooperation with Cabinet arrangements, where leaders from different parties sit together in weekly meetings.
He said Loke had blurred the distinction between governance responsibilities and political alignment.
"Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's presence in Cabinet meetings is in his official capacity as a federal government leader, where seating arrangements and participation are determined by constitutional responsibility, not political preference.
"In carrying out national duties, a leader does not choose who sits beside him in Cabinet.
"However, in political cooperation, every party has the right to decide its partners based on principles, ideology and values," he said in a statement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No cover-up in Taiping prison death case, says Saifuddin
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government will not shield any wrongdoing uncovered in the death of an inmate at Taiping Prison last year, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said.
He said authorities must allow due process to take its course if investigations establish clear evidence of misconduct.
"Our principle is very clear. If a case has gone through an investigation process, regardless of which body conducted it, and compelling evidence is presented, with all relevant processes carried out and witnesses interviewed, then a follow-up investigation must proceed," he said at the Home Ministry's monthly assembly meeting today.
He said the matter was recently discussed at Cabinet level following an inquiry report by the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam), which claimed multiple discrepancies and wrongdoing by the prison's management.
He said the government's position remained firm on accountability.
"What is right is right, what is wrong is wrong, and it will not be defended."
Saifuddin said he believed Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail would continue or complete any necessary investigations while taking into account Suhakam's findings.
"Speaking the truth can be painful, but the long-term impact is much better.
"Plus, this matter is also a question of integrity within our organisation, which must be addressed accordingly," he said.
However, Saifuddin added that the incident should not overshadow the broader achievements of the Prisons Department, which he described as among the ministry's success stories.
"These are success stories and just because of an isolated case such as what happened in Kamunting, we do not want public perception to change."
Last month, it was reported that Suhakam released its findings and called for immediate disciplinary action against Taiping Prison officers involved.
Findings by Suhakam's inquiry panel on the Jan 17, 2025 incident were delivered by chairman Datuk Seri Mohd Hishamuddin Md Yunus.
He said
disciplinary action
was warranted based on surveillance footage showing evidence of misconduct.
The public inquiry panel also found that the Prisons Department failed to take disciplinary action against the Taiping Prison officers involved, even though the Board of Inquiry found that standard operating procedures had been violated.
On Dec 19, 2025, prison warden Ryndee O'Nel Victor, 25, was charged with causing the death of inmate Gan Chin Eng, 62.
He claimed trial under Section 304(b) of the Penal Code for culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
Suhakam identified several deep-rooted systemic issues as underlying causes of the tragedy.
They include severe prison overcrowding, a lack of clear planning in transferring detainees from the Kamunting Correctional Centre to Taiping Prison, general administrative weaknesses, a shortage of prison staff, excessively long remand detention periods, personnel conduct, and the dilapidated and hazardous state of the facility, such as the continued use of the outdated bucket toilet system.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six territorial army camps to host PLKN 4.0, train 10,520 recruits next year
Article:
PEKAN: The National Service Training Programme (PLKN) 4.0 will undergo a major expansion next year, with 10,520 participants expected across four series compared with 4,200 recruits this year.
National Service Training Department director-general Major-General Datuk Marzuki Mokhtar said the programme, currently conducted at three camps, would be expanded to six Territorial Army camps nationwide.
"Series Two, which ended today, involved three camps, namely in Kuala Lumpur, Pekan and Malbatt Village in Negri Sembilan. In the third series, scheduled for July, Malbatt Village will be replaced by the 504th Territorial Army Regiment Camp in Bukit Keteri, Perlis.
"The fourth series in October will see recruits enrolling at three additional Territorial Army camps in Gemas, Negri Sembilan, Sungai Durian, Kelantan, and Jitra, Kedah, bringing the total number of operational camps to six," he told reporters at the 505th Territorial Army Regiment Camp here today.
Earlier, Marzuki witnessed the passing-out parade of 531 male trainees who began their 45-day training on April 25.
He said the opening of the new camps later this year would be carried out in stages, depending on their availability and the completion of upgrading works.
Marzuki said all six camps were expected to be fully operational next year and capable of accommodating about 10,520 trainees, with the figure likely to increase to 11,000 across the four series.
He said 1,369 trainees completed Series Two today across three camps: Pekan for male recruits, Kuala Lumpur for 245 female recruits, and Malbatt Village for 593 recruits.
Meanwhile, Marzuki said the department planned to organise roadshows and visit rural areas to promote the 45-day training programme, as many people were still unaware of it.
"We have found that most recruits come from urban areas with better internet access, while participation from rural communities remains relatively low. We plan to visit schools, distribute pamphlets and put up posters to increase awareness of the programme.
"The department aims to provide the community, especially parents, with accurate information about the programme and increase public awareness of its existence," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police clarify younger brother was driving car in Sungai Petani crash
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Police have clarified that the Proton X50 involved in yesterday's crash that killed six family members was driven by its owner, Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri, 21, and not his elder brother as initially reported.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the earlier identification was based on preliminary information and confusion caused by the severely mangled condition of the vehicle.
Initial reports had named police corporal Ahmad Shafiq Ahmad Shukri, 29, as the driver.
However, further investigations confirmed that Ahmad Fahim was behind the wheel at the time of the crash.
Adzli said Ahmad Fahim had only collected the newly purchased SUV yesterday, just hours before the fatal accident.
In the 3.50pm crash at Kampung Baru Pinang Tunggal, six family members were killed when the SUV collided with a soil-laden lorry.
There were seven people in the car. The sole survivor of the crash, three-year-old Aulia Sofea Ahmad Shafiq, remains hospitalised with serious injuries.
Five victims were laid to rest at the Al Abidi Mosque Muslim Cemetery in Merbok this morning, while seven-year-old Iskandar Affan Ibrahim was buried earlier at the Permatang Kuang Mosque Muslim Cemetery in Penaga, Penang.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Malaysia ready to send aid after 7.9-magnitude Philippines quake [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia stands ready to assist the Philippines following a powerful 7.9-magnitude earthquake that struck off its southern region this morning.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed deep concern over the disaster, saying Malaysia's thoughts and prayers were with the Filipino people.
"Our thoughts and prayers are with the Filipino people. Malaysia stands ready to render whatever assistance may be required," he said in a Facebook post today.
Anwar noted that tremors from the earthquake were felt in parts of Sabah, assuring that the government was closely monitoring the situation with emergency services placed on full alert.
"Malaysia and the Philippines are close friends and Asean partners. I pray for the safety and well-being of all those affected, wishing them strength and courage in the difficult days ahead," he said.
The
earthquake
struck Mindanao at 7.37am today at a depth of 50km, killing at least one person, damaging buildings and triggering tsunami warnings across the region.
In Sabah, the Fire and Rescue Department reported
no structural damage
, unusual sea level rise or emergency incidents following a subsequent tsunami advisory.
The department said monitoring operations were carried out in coastal areas, residential settlements and high-risk locations in Lahad Datu, Tawau, Kunak and Semporna.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Iskandar left me on my birthday,' says grieving father
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: A father is mourning the loss of his 7-year-old son, who was among six family members killed in a horrific crash, just hours after celebrating his birthday.
Ibrahim Ghazali, 39, said his son, Iskandar Affan Ibrahim, had spent his final day singing birthday songs and presenting him with a gift before the tragedy struck in Kampung Baru Pinang Tunggal here yesterday.
"Iskandar left me with a memory on my birthday by leaving me forever.
"I have accepted his passing, although he was my only son," he said when met at the Al Abidi Mosque Muslim Cemetery in Merbok today.
Ibrahim said Iskandar had been especially cheerful and excited about riding in a newly purchased Proton X50 before the accident.
"He repeatedly wished me happy birthday, sang birthday songs and gave me a present that his mother had bought last Friday.
"That was the last birthday greeting and song I received from him," he said.
The family had gathered at his home in Taman Bertam Indah, Penang, for a birthday celebration before the outing that ended in tragedy.
Despite losing his only son, Ibrahim said he felt even greater sympathy for his wife, who lost her mother, two brothers and her son in the crash.
Meanwhile, Iskandar's aunt, Siti Nur Atikah Ahmad Shukri, said she never imagined losing her mother, Nora Mohd Husin, 55; her brothers Ahmad Shafiq, 29, and Ahmad Fahim, 21; sister-in-law Jamaliah Sannusi, 29; nephew Ahmad Mikail Ahmad Shafiq, two months; and Iskandar in a single tragedy.
The 40-year-old said she was still coming to terms with the loss, having also lost her father, Ahmad Shukri Osman, in February 2024.
"In the blink of an eye, I lost my mother, my two brothers and my nephew. We were all very close, even though we did not meet often," she said.
She said the family members had been travelling to Merbok to visit their late father's grave when the crash occurred.
"We never imagined that today they would be buried not far from our father's grave," she said.
Earlier, Nora, Ahmad Fahim, Ahmad Shafiq, Jamaliah and Ahmad Mikail were laid to rest in a shared grave at about 10am, while Iskandar was buried at the Permatang Kuang Mosque Muslim Cemetery in Penaga, Penang, at about 2am.
Siti Nur Atikah said Iskandar had become especially affectionate towards her in recent months and had been thrilled to join his uncles in the new Proton X50, which had been collected only hours before the accident.
"He was very interested in the new car and was so excited to follow his uncle after the vehicle was collected at about 11am yesterday, just a few hours before the crash," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: FT minister seeks end to Special OSC in Kuala Lumpur planning approvals over interference concerns
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Federal Territories minister said she will no longer convene Kuala Lumpur’s Special One Stop Centre (OSC) and will seek Cabinet approval to abolish the body, saying it could create room for interference or the perception of interference in development decisions.
In a statement today, Hannah Yeoh said the move forms part of the government’s broader governance reform agenda aimed at strengthening integrity and transparency in Kuala Lumpur’s urban planning system.
The decision follows recommendations by Project Capital, a special task force under the Policy Advisory Committee to the Prime Minister (PMAC), which was tasked by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim with reviewing governance challenges in the federal capital.
According to the task force’s report, the Special OSC had the potential to become a channel for intervention in the development approval process.
“The Madani Government cannot defend any structure that creates room for interference or the perception that a decision can be influenced by factors other than legitimate laws and planning policies,” Yeoh said.
“That is why we want to return to a clear principle: follow the Local Plan, follow the law and follow a transparent process.”
Yeoh said she had decided not to convene the Special OSC for as long as she remains Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories), while also proposing that the Cabinet consider formally abolishing the mechanism in line with Project Capital’s recommendations.
The minister said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) had already introduced measures aimed at improving transparency and accountability in planning approvals.
These include a dedicated OSC portal that allows stakeholders and members of the public to monitor development applications currently being processed.
Yeoh said DBKL had also streamlined its internal procedures to ensure that applications are assessed based on the Kuala Lumpur Local Plan 2040.
Under the revised process, development applications that fully comply with stipulated requirements can be approved in as little as 21 days.
For more complex proposals requiring further consideration, consultations with government agencies, departments, organisations and other stakeholders will continue under existing planning regulations.
Yeoh also stressed that urban planning decisions should remain in the hands of qualified officials rather than be subject to external influence.
“Every development decision needs to be made by experienced officers based on assessments of technical documents, comments from relevant departments and the approved Kuala Lumpur Local Plan,” she said.
“Urban planning decisions must be made based on facts, policy and law, not influenced by any party.”
She said abolishing the Special OSC would remove unnecessary layers of bureaucracy, speed up decision-making and provide clearer accountability within DBKL’s administration.
Yeoh also thanked Anwar for establishing Project Capital, saying its recommendations reflected the government’s commitment to strengthening governance, improving accountability and carrying out institutional reforms for Kuala Lumpur’s future.

Title: Music teacher in Klang pleads not guilty to 14 charges of sexual assault on six students
Article:
KLANG, June 8 — A male teacher pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to 14 charges of committing physical sexual assault against six 15-year-old female students at a secondary school in Sabak Bernam.
The accused, Idi Johan Mohd Zohdi, 45, a music teacher, entered his plea after all charges were read out before Judge Sharifah Hascindie Syed Omar.
According to the charges, the man allegedly committed various acts of physical sexual assault against the six female students.
The alleged incidents took place at several locations, including a classroom, music room, mathematics room, music equipment store, a house, and a vehicle in Sekinchan and Sabak Bernam between January 2025 and February of this year.
The charges were filed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which carries imprisonment of up to 20 years and caning.
Additionally, under Section 16(1), he faces an additional prison term of up to five years and no fewer than two strokes of the cane, upon conviction.
Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Nor Suhaili Abu Bakar did not offer bail. But if the court were to grant it, she proposed RM20,000 per charge, along with conditions that the accused not harass the victims and report monthly to a police station.
The accused’s lawyer, Datuk Mohd Imran Tamrin, requested that the bail amount be reduced, citing that his client has a wife and a school-going child as dependents, and has no prior criminal record.
He added that the accused, who has served as a teacher since 2002, has never faced any disciplinary action throughout his career.
The court then allowed the accused to be released on bail of RM30,000 for all charges, subject to the additional conditions proposed by the prosecution.
He was also ordered to surrender his passport. The court then set July 30 for the next case mention. — Bernama

Title: Shrimp ban sparks tension as Malaysia demands answers, Thailand threatens WTO escalation over import curbs
Article:
KUALA NERUS, June 8 — Malaysia is awaiting Thailand’s response to a questionnaire on shrimp imports before assessing the country’s compliance with local biosecurity requirements following the ongoing trade dispute involving the commodity.
Fisheries Director-General Datuk Adnan Hussain said the Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security is currently examining the matter and has requested Thailand to respond to the questionnaire submitted through Malaysia’s agricultural representative office in Bangkok.
“We have asked the Thai authorities to answer the questionnaire we sent regarding shrimp imports so that they can provide the necessary clarification.
“Once we receive the response, we will assess whether it meets Malaysia’s biosecurity requirements,” he told reporters after the World Ocean Day 2026 celebration at Pulau Redang Marine Park here today.
Adnan said Malaysia has also strengthened its biosecurity measures on imports of sea bass from Thailand following Bangkok’s decision to impose restrictions and conduct full inspections on Malaysian sea bass exports.
He said Malaysia now requires a Certificate of Analysis (CoA) for sea bass imported from Thailand before the product is allowed into the country.
“Our sea bass exports are also subjected to restrictions and comprehensive inspections by Thailand. As such, we are applying similar biosecurity measures,” he said.
When asked whether Thailand had been given a deadline to respond, Adnan said no specific timeframe had been set.
“We have not imposed any deadline at this stage. Our priority is to conduct a thorough evaluation once we receive a complete response from Thailand,” he said.
On May 16, Malaysia tightened import controls on fishery products from Thailand by enforcing CoA requirements for sea bass and imposing a temporary ban on five species of shrimp effective June 1.
The affected shrimp species are Penaeus esculentes, Fenneropenaeus merguiensis, Penaeus vannamei, Penaeus monodon and Penaeus stylirostris.
Yesterday, it was reported Thailand is prepared to raise the issue of Malaysia’s temporary suspension of imports of the five shrimp species at the World Trade Organisation (WTO) and Asean forums should bilateral negotiations fail to resolve the matter. — Bernama

Title: Ex‑Army chief’s wife’s jewellery seized under MACC Act, court warns assets forfeited if no claims
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Sessions Court issued a third-party notice today to anyone wishing to challenge the forfeiture of jewellery seized from Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin, wife of former Malaysian Army chief Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan.
Judge Azura Alwi set July 31 for the hearing of any claims from individuals with an interest in the jewellery. If no third party comes forward, the assets will be forfeited to the government.
The third-party notice was issued under Section 41(2) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009 after the court allowed an application by deputy public prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat.
Lawyer Muhammad Kausar Mohd Khairi, representing Salwani, 27, confirmed that his client had no objection to the application.
According to the notice filed on May 4, the prosecution is seeking forfeiture to the government of a 29.17-gramme necklace, a 9.53-gramme bracelet, a pair of earrings weighing 6.61 grammes, and a Swarovski ring.
The movable assets were seized under Subsection 41(1) of the MACC Act 2009 and are currently in the custody of the MACC.
On January 22, Salwani was charged with four counts of receiving money from illegal activities, amounting to RM50,000, RM7,000, RM10,000 and RM10,000.
The money was allegedly deposited into a company bank account at a bank in Damansara Heights, Wisma UOA II here, between November 24, 2024, and November 25, 2025.
The charges were filed under Subsection 4(1)(b) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act, which is punishable by imprisonment for up to 15 years and a fine of not less than 5 times the value of the illicit proceeds or RM5 million, whichever is higher.
Salwani was also charged at the Kuala Terengganu Sessions Court on January 26 with receiving RM5,000, also proceeds from illegal activities, allegedly deposited into her account at a bank in Jertih, near Besut, on January 16, 2025.
That case has since been transferred to the Sessions Court here to be tried jointly. — Bernama

Title: PM Anwar heads to Japan to meet Takaichi, push new Malaysia‑Japan cooperation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim departed for Tokyo today to lead the Malaysian delegation on the Madani Diplomacy mission to strengthen strategic cooperation and national energy security, as the world faces a global energy supply crisis.
In a Facebook post, Anwar said that during the visit, he will hold a meeting with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to discuss the development of bilateral relations and to pioneer new Malaysia-Japan cooperation, particularly in the fields of economy, investment and energy security.
The Prime Minister said that he will also deliver a keynote speech at the 31st Nikkei Forum: Future of Asia, as well as a special lecture at the University of Tokyo on the challenges and future of this region in an increasingly challenging global landscape.
“Pray that our mission runs smoothly and brings beneficial results for the people and the country,” he posted.
Yesterday, the Foreign Ministry in a statement announced that Anwar’s official visit to Japan will begin today until June 10 at the invitation of the Japanese government.
Japan continues to be one of Malaysia’s most important economic partners, ranking as the country’s fifth-largest trading partner in the world since 2024. — Bernama

Title: Tumpat court fines seven over attack on driving instructor linked to alleged derogatory remarks about student
Article:
